patch:
diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/EarlyCSE.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/EarlyCSE.cpp
index 09cb2f4cb010..106bf3166010 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/EarlyCSE.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Scalar/EarlyCSE.cpp
@@ -397,6 +397,18 @@ static bool isEqualImpl(SimpleValue LHS, SimpleValue RHS) {
   auto *RII = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(RHSI);
   if (LII && RII && LII->getIntrinsicID() == RII->getIntrinsicID() &&
       LII->isCommutative() && LII->arg_size() >= 2) {
+    // When matching commutative intrinsics, also ensure that their attribute
+    // lists can be intersected. The identical check above already enforced
+    // this for non-commuted operand order. Without this guard, EarlyCSE may
+    // consider calls equivalent and later fail to intersect attributes in
+    // combineIRFlags(), triggering an assertion. This mirrors the behavior
+    // used in other value numbering (e.g. GVN) which rejects non-intersectable
+    // callsites.
+    if ((!LII->getAttributes().isEmpty() || !RII->getAttributes().isEmpty()) &&
+        !LII->getAttributes()
+             .intersectWith(LII->getContext(), RII->getAttributes())
+             .has_value())
+      return false;
     return LII->getArgOperand(0) == RII->getArgOperand(1) &&
            LII->getArgOperand(1) == RII->getArgOperand(0) &&
            std::equal(LII->arg_begin() + 2, LII->arg_end(),
test_traj:

+             .intersectWith(LII->getContext(), RII->getAttributes())
+             .has_value())
+      return false;
     return LII->getArgOperand(0) == RII->getArgOperand(1) &&
            LII->getArgOperand(1) == RII->getArgOperand(0) &&
            std::equal(LII->arg_begin() + 2, LII->arg_end(),
